metadata.naval_headquarters: Integrated Headquarters of Ministry of Defence (Navy)
metadata.motto: Sham No Varunah (May the Lord of the Oceans be auspicious unto us)
metadata.ensign: White Ensign with Saint George's Cross and National Emblem

general_facts.strategic_importance: Guards 7,516 km coastline and island territories
general_facts.funfact: The Indian Navy is the only navy in the world to have sunk an enemy warship using an anti-ship missile in combat (INS Nipat sank PNS Khaibar in 1971).

commands.name: Western Naval Command
commands.area_of_responsibility: Arabian Sea, West Coast of India
commands.funfact: The largest of all three commands. Houses the aircraft carrier INS Vikramaditya.

commands.name: Eastern Naval Command
commands.area_of_responsibility: Bay of Bengal, East Coast of India, Andaman & Nicobar
commands.funfact: Controls the strategic Andaman & Nicobar Command, India's only tri-service command.

commands.name: Southern Naval Command
commands.funfact: Primary training command of the Indian Navy. Hosts the prestigious Indian Naval Academy at Ezhimala.

naval_bases.name: INS Kadamba
naval_bases.funfact: Asia's largest naval base! Named 'Project Seabird', it can berth over 50 warships including aircraft carriers and nuclear submarines. Cost over ₹25,000 crores to build.

naval_bases.name: INS Shikra
naval_bases.funfact: Strategically located in Mumbai Harbor. Houses helicopters for the Western Fleet.

naval_bases.name: INS Dega
naval_bases.funfact: Primary air station for Eastern Naval Command. Home to maritime patrol aircraft.

naval_bases.name: INS Hansa
naval_bases.funfact: India's largest naval air station! Operates MiG-29K fighters. Training base for carrier-based pilots.

naval_bases.name: INS Jarawa
naval_bases.funfact: Strategic base in the Andaman Islands, guarding the approaches to the Strait of Malacca.

naval_bases.name: INS Chilka
naval_bases.funfact: One of Asia's largest sailor training establishments. Located on the banks of Chilika Lake.

naval_bases.name: Indian Naval Academy (INA)
naval_bases.funfact: Asia's largest naval academy! Built on 2,500 acres. Trains officers from India and friendly foreign nations. Cost ₹3,000 crores to build.

aircraft_carriers.name: INS Vikramaditya
aircraft_carriers.funfact: Purchased from Russia for $2.35 billion - the most expensive military purchase in Indian history at that time. Originally laid down in 1978 as a Soviet carrier. Has a distinctive ski-jump ramp for aircraft takeoff.

aircraft_carriers.name: INS Vikrant
aircraft_carriers.funfact: India's first indigenous aircraft carrier and largest warship ever built in India! Named after the original INS Vikrant (R11) that served in the 1971 war. Makes India only the 6th country to build its own aircraft carrier. 76% indigenous content.

aircraft_carriers_decommissioned.name: INS Vikrant (R11) - Original
aircraft_carriers_decommissioned.funfact: India's first aircraft carrier! Played crucial role in 1971 war, blockading East Pakistan (now Bangladesh). Her aircraft flew 125+ sorties. Now a museum in Mumbai (planned).

aircraft_carriers_decommissioned.name: INS Viraat (R22)
aircraft_carriers_decommissioned.service_history: 30 years in Indian Navy (59 years total)
aircraft_carriers_decommissioned.funfact: The longest-serving aircraft carrier in the world! Served in the Royal Navy during the Falklands War before joining India. Operated Sea Harrier jets. Plans to convert to museum/hotel.

active.name: INS Visakhapatnam
active.funfact: Lead ship of India's most advanced indigenous destroyers! 75% indigenous content. Has advanced stealth features reducing radar signature. Can simultaneously track and engage multiple aerial and surface targets.

active.name: INS Mormugao
active.funfact: Named after the port city of Mormugao in Goa. Second ship of Project 15B. Features improved stealth and combat systems compared to older destroyers.

active.name: INS Imphal
active.funfact: Named after Imphal, capital of Manipur. Third ship of the class. Features enhanced automation allowing smaller crew.

active.name: INS Surat
active.funfact: Named after the commercial capital of Gujarat. Fourth and final ship of Project 15B class.

active.name: INS Kolkata
active.funfact: Lead ship of India's first indigenously designed stealth destroyers! The most potent surface combatants in the Indian Navy. Can engage targets 300+ km away with BrahMos missiles.

active.name: INS Kochi
active.funfact: Named after the beautiful port city of Kochi. Has the capability to detect and track over 100 targets simultaneously.

active.name: INS Chennai
active.funfact: Third and final ship of the Kolkata-class. Named after Chennai, the capital of Tamil Nadu.

active.name: INS Delhi
active.funfact: Lead ship of India's first indigenous destroyer class! Represented a quantum leap in Indian warship design. Being upgraded with Barak-8 missiles.

active.name: INS Mysore
active.funfact: Named after the historic city of Mysore. Participated in numerous international naval exercises and anti-piracy operations.

active.name: INS Mumbai
active.funfact: Named after India's commercial capital. The last of the Delhi-class destroyers.

decommissioned_destroyers.name: INS Rajput
decommissioned_destroyers.funfact: Lead ship of the Rajput-class and first destroyer of Soviet origin in the Indian Navy. Served for 41 years! Participated in Operation Pawan in Sri Lanka.

decommissioned_destroyers.name: INS Rana
decommissioned_destroyers.funfact: Named after the Rana warrior clan. Served extensively in the Indian Ocean region.

decommissioned_destroyers.name: INS Ranjit
decommissioned_destroyers.funfact: Named meaning 'Victor in Battle'. Served for 36 years.

decommissioned_destroyers.name: INS Ranvir
decommissioned_destroyers.funfact: The first Rajput-class ship equipped with BrahMos missiles after modernization.

decommissioned_destroyers.name: INS Ranvijay
decommissioned_destroyers.funfact: The last surviving Rajput-class destroyer in active service. May be decommissioned soon.

active.name: INS Shivalik
active.funfact: Lead ship of India's first indigenous stealth frigates! Features 'X-form' hull for reduced radar signature. Named after the Shivalik mountain range.

active.name: INS Satpura
active.funfact: Named after the Satpura mountain range in central India.

active.name: INS Sahyadri
active.funfact: Named after the Sahyadri (Western Ghats) mountain range. Third and final ship of the class.

active.name: INS Nilgiri
active.funfact: Lead ship of India's most advanced frigates! Enhanced stealth features compared to Shivalik-class. 7 ships ordered (4 from MDL, 3 from GRSE). Will form the backbone of the future navy.

active.name: INS Udaygiri
active.funfact: Named after a hill in Odisha with ancient Buddhist caves.

active.name: INS Taragiri
active.funfact: Named after a hill in Maharashtra.

active.name: INS Vindhyagiri
active.funfact: Named after the Vindhya mountain range.

active.name: INS Himgiri
active.funfact: First of three Project 17A frigates built at GRSE. Named after the Himalayan range.

active.name: INS Dunagiri
active.funfact: Named after a peak in Uttarakhand.

active.name: Ship 7 (Unnamed)
active.funfact: Seventh and final ship of the Project 17A class.

active.name: INS Talwar
active.funfact: Lead ship of the most numerous frigate class in the Indian Navy! 'Talwar' means 'Sword'. These ships are the workhorses of the navy.

active.name: INS Trishul
active.funfact: 'Trishul' means Lord Shiva's Trident.

active.name: INS Tabar
active.funfact: 'Tabar' means Battle Axe. Famous for sinking a pirate 'mother ship' in the Gulf of Aden in 2008!

active.name: INS Teg
active.funfact: 'Teg' means 'Sabre'. Second batch with improved systems.

active.name: INS Tarkash
active.funfact: 'Tarkash' means 'Quiver'. Equipped with BrahMos missiles.

active.name: INS Trikand
active.funfact: 'Trikand' is another name for Trident. Last of the second batch.

active.name: INS Brahmaputra
active.funfact: Lead ship named after the mighty Brahmaputra River. Built with stealth features. Capsized at Naval Dockyard Mumbai in July 2024 during maintenance.

active.name: INS Betwa
active.funfact: Named after the Betwa River in central India.

active.name: INS Beas
active.funfact: Named after the Beas River in Punjab. Third and final ship of the class.

active.name: INS Kamorta
active.funfact: India's first indigenous stealth ASW corvette! Named after Kamorta Island in Andaman & Nicobar. Features advanced sonar systems and torpedo capabilities. First warship with superstructure of carbon fiber composite.

active.name: INS Kadmatt
active.funfact: Named after Kadmat Island in Lakshadweep.

active.name: INS Kiltan
active.funfact: Named after Kiltan Island in Lakshadweep. First warship with superstructure entirely of carbon fiber.

active.name: INS Kavaratti
active.funfact: Named after Kavaratti, capital of Lakshadweep. Fourth and final ship of the class.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Nilgiri (F33) - Original
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: India's first indigenous frigate! Based on British Leander-class design. Paved the way for Indian warship building.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Himgiri (F34) - Original
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: Second ship of the original Nilgiri-class.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Dunagiri (F35) - Original
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: Third ship of the original Nilgiri-class.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Godavari
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: Lead ship of India's first fully indigenous frigate design. Named after the Godavari River.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Ganga
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: Named after the holy River Ganga.

frigates_decommissioned.name: INS Gomati
frigates_decommissioned.funfact: Last of the Godavari-class. Named after the Gomti River. Participated in Operation Cactus (Maldives, 1988).

active.name: INS Kora
active.funfact: Lead ship of four Kora-class corvettes. Packed with 16 anti-ship missiles despite small size!

active.name: INS Kirch
active.funfact: Named after a sword.

active.name: INS Kulish
active.funfact: 'Kulish' means thunderbolt.

active.name: INS Khanjar
active.funfact: 'Khanjar' means dagger. Last of the Kora-class.

active.name: INS Khukri
active.funfact: Named after the legendary Gurkha knife 'Khukri'. Also honors the original INS Khukri sunk in 1971 war.

active.name: INS Kuthar
active.funfact: 'Kuthar' means battle-axe.

active.name: INS Kirpan
active.funfact: 'Kirpan' means sword/dagger (sacred to Sikhs).

active.name: INS Karmuk
active.funfact: One of the surviving Veer-class corvettes. Fast attack craft with potent anti-ship capability.

corvettes_decommissioned.name: INS Veer
corvettes_decommissioned.funfact: Lead ship of 12 Tarantul-class corvettes transferred from the Soviet Union. Most now decommissioned.

corvettes_decommissioned.name: INS Khukri (F149) - Original
corvettes_decommissioned.funfact: Sunk by Pakistani submarine PNS Hangor during 1971 war - the first submarine kill since WWII. 18 officers and 176 sailors lost. The Captain chose to go down with his ship.

ssbn_ballistic_missile.name: INS Arihant
armament: 12 x K-15 Sagarika SLBMs (750 km range) OR
ssbn_ballistic_missile.funfact: India's FIRST indigenous nuclear submarine! Completes India's nuclear triad. Named 'Arihant' meaning 'Destroyer of Enemies'. Part of the secret Advanced Technology Vessel (ATV) project started in 1984. Gives India second-strike nuclear capability.

ssbn_ballistic_missile.name: INS Arighat
ssbn_ballistic_missile.funfact: Second indigenous SSBN. 'Arighat' means 'Destroyer of Enemies'. Has improved systems compared to Arihant. Strengthens India's nuclear deterrence.

ssbn_ballistic_missile.name: S4 (Unnamed)
ssbn_ballistic_missile.funfact: Third SSBN under construction. Believed to be larger with more missile tubes. Will carry K-4 missiles with 3,500 km range.

ssbn_ballistic_missile.name: S4* (Unnamed)
ssbn_ballistic_missile.funfact: Next-generation SSBN class reportedly under development. Will be significantly larger and carry more powerful missiles.

ssn_attack.name: INS Chakra II
ssn_attack.funfact: India's first nuclear attack submarine (leased)! Provided valuable training for Indian submariners. The 10-year lease cost $900 million. Allowed India to operate nuclear subs before indigenous ones were ready.

ssn_attack.name: INS Chakra I (Original)
ssn_attack.funfact: India's FIRST nuclear submarine ever! Leased from the Soviet Union. Gave India early experience in nuclear submarine operations.

ssn_attack.name: INS Chakra III
ssn_attack.funfact: India is negotiating a third lease of Russian nuclear attack submarine while indigenous SSN program progresses.

ssn_attack.name: Indigenous SSN (Project Varsha)
ssn_attack.funfact: India's ambitious program to build 6 indigenous nuclear attack submarines. Will be larger than Arihant-class. Part of the 30-year submarine building plan.

diesel_electric.name: INS Kalvari
diesel_electric.funfact: Lead ship of India's most advanced conventional submarines! 'Kalvari' means Tiger Shark. Built under technology transfer from France (Naval Group). Features advanced stealth and can stay submerged for weeks.

diesel_electric.name: INS Khanderi
diesel_electric.funfact: Named after the Maratha island fortress 'Khanderi' built by Shivaji. Second Scorpène submarine.

diesel_electric.name: INS Karanj
diesel_electric.funfact: Named after Karanja Island near Mumbai. Third submarine of the class.

diesel_electric.name: INS Vela
diesel_electric.funfact: Named after the Vela constellation (The Sails). Fourth submarine.

diesel_electric.name: INS Vagir
diesel_electric.funfact: 'Vagir' is a sand fish found in Indian Ocean. Fifth submarine of the class.

diesel_electric.name: INS Vagsheer
diesel_electric.funfact: 'Vagsheer' means 'Lord of the Deep'. Sixth and final Scorpène submarine. Being fitted with indigenous AIP system.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhukesari
diesel_electric.funfact: One of the surviving Kilo-class submarines. Extremely quiet and nicknamed 'Black Hole' by US Navy for its stealth. Being replaced by Scorpènes.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhuraj
diesel_electric.funfact: One of the older but still capable Kilo-class submarines.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhushastra
diesel_electric.funfact: The last Kilo-class submarine acquired. Newer than others with upgraded systems.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhukirti
diesel_electric.funfact: Underwent extensive refit to extend service life.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhuvijay
diesel_electric.funfact: Underwent major refit in Russia, returned with upgraded systems.

diesel_electric.name: INS Sindhudhvaj
diesel_electric.funfact: Served for over 35 years. Successfully launched Klub missile during tests.

diesel_electric.name: INS Shishumar
diesel_electric.funfact: Lead ship of the German-origin submarines. 'Shishumar' means Crocodile/Dolphin. Two built in Germany, two in India.

diesel_electric.name: INS Shankush
diesel_electric.funfact: Built in Germany. 'Shankush' means Conch Shell.

diesel_electric.name: INS Shalki
diesel_electric.funfact: First submarine built in India using German technology transfer!

diesel_electric.name: INS Shankul
diesel_electric.funfact: Second and last submarine built in India under German collaboration.

submarines_decommissioned.name: INS Kursura
submarines_decommissioned.funfact: Now a submarine museum in Visakhapatnam! One of India's first submarines. Thousands of visitors tour it annually.

submarines_decommissioned.name: INS Vagli
submarines_decommissioned.funfact: Served for over 35 years in the Indian Navy.

submarines_decommissioned.name: INS Sindhurakshak
submarines_decommissioned.funfact: Tragically lost in an explosion and fire at Mumbai Naval Dockyard. 18 sailors died. The submarine sank in the dock.

submarines_decommissioned.name: INS Sindhuratna
submarines_decommissioned.funfact: Suffered a serious fire in 2014, declared beyond repair and decommissioned.

future_submarines.name: Project 75(I)
future_submarines.features: Air Independent Propulsion (AIP), Land-attack missiles, Advanced stealth
future_submarines.funfact: India's largest-ever defense procurement program! Will cost approximately ₹43,000 crores. Will be built in India with foreign collaboration.

future_submarines.name: Project Varsha/Indigenous SSN
future_submarines.funfact: India's ambitious plan to build indigenous nuclear attack submarines. Will give India blue-water capability.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Saryu
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Lead ship of Saryu-class. Designed for EEZ patrol, anti-piracy, and fleet support.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Sumedha
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Named after a river mentioned in ancient texts.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Suvarna
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Fourth and final ship of the Saryu-class.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Sharda
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: First of the Sukanya-class OPVs. Partially built in South Korea.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Sukanya
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Lead ship of the class. Named after a character from Hindu mythology.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Subhadra
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Named after Subhadra, sister of Lord Krishna.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Suvarna (Original)
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Built entirely in India unlike earlier ships of the class.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Savitri
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Named after the legendary queen Savitri.

offshore_patrol_vessels.name: INS Sujata
offshore_patrol_vessels.funfact: Named after Sujata who fed Buddha rice pudding.

fast_attack_craft.name: INS Car Nicobar
fast_attack_craft.funfact: Lead ship of the class. Named after Car Nicobar island. Designed for coastal patrol and fast interception.

fast_attack_craft.name: INS Chetlat
fast_attack_craft.funfact: Named after Chetlat Island in Lakshadweep.

fast_attack_craft.name: INS Cheriyam
fast_attack_craft.funfact: Named after Cheriyam Island in Lakshadweep.

fast_attack_craft.name: INS Kavaratti (FAC)
fast_attack_craft.funfact: Named after Kavaratti Island in Lakshadweep.

fast_attack_craft.name: INS Kalpeni
fast_attack_craft.funfact: Named after Kalpeni Island in Lakshadweep.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Jalashwa
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: India's largest amphibious ship! Acquired from US Navy for $48 million. Can land troops anywhere along enemy coast. Used in multiple evacuation operations including Yemen (Operation Raahat).

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Shardul
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: Lead ship of indigenous LST class. Can beach itself to unload troops and tanks directly onto shore.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Kesari
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: 'Kesari' means Saffron - the color of courage in Indian culture.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Airavat
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: Named after Airavata, the white elephant mount of Lord Indra.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Magar
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: 'Magar' means Crocodile. First indigenous LST. Participated in IPKF operations in Sri Lanka.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INS Gharial
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: Named after the endangered Gharial crocodile.

amphibious_warfare_ships.name: INLCU L51-L58
amphibious_warfare_ships.funfact: Eight LCUs based in Andaman & Nicobar. Can carry troops and tanks directly to beaches. Critical for island defense.

tankers_and_replenishment.name: INS Deepak
tankers_and_replenishment.funfact: Can refuel multiple ships simultaneously while underway at sea. Essential for extended blue-water operations.

tankers_and_replenishment.name: INS Shakti
tankers_and_replenishment.funfact: 'Shakti' means Power. Second of the modern fleet tankers.

tankers_and_replenishment.name: INS Aditya
tankers_and_replenishment.funfact: First large auxiliary ship built in India. Named after the Sun God.

survey_and_research_vessels.name: INS Sandhayak
survey_and_research_vessels.funfact: Lead ship of indigenous survey vessels. Has charted vast areas of Indian Ocean.

survey_and_research_vessels.name: INS Sarvekshak
survey_and_research_vessels.funfact: New generation survey vessel with modern equipment. Name means 'Surveyor'. First of four planned vessels.

survey_and_research_vessels.name: INS Makar
survey_and_research_vessels.funfact: Unique catamaran design provides stability for survey operations.

survey_and_research_vessels.name: INS Sagardhwani
survey_and_research_vessels.funfact: Operated by Naval Physical & Oceanographic Laboratory (NPOL). Conducts anti-submarine warfare research. 'Sagardhwani' means 'Sound of the Ocean'.

training_ships.name: INS Tir
training_ships.funfact: 'Tir' means Arrow. Trains naval cadets on long voyages. Has circumnavigated the globe!

training_ships.name: INS Tarangini
training_ships.funfact: India's first sail training ship! Built entirely of wood. Has sailed over 200,000 nautical miles. Participated in tall ship races worldwide. Name means 'Riding the Waves'.

training_ships.name: INS Sudarshini
training_ships.funfact: Sister ship of Tarangini. Named after Sudarshana Chakra, Lord Vishnu's divine discus.

training_ships.name: INS Dronacharya
training_ships.funfact: Named after the legendary archer teacher from Mahabharata. Trains sailors in weapon systems.

diving_support_vessels.name: INS Nireekshak
diving_support_vessels.funfact: 'Nireekshak' means Observer. Can rescue trapped submarine crew. Has participated in multiple salvage operations.

mine_warfare_vessels.name: INS Kozhikode
mine_warfare_vessels.funfact: Lead ship of indigenous minesweepers. Wooden/GRP hull to avoid triggering magnetic mines.

mine_warfare_vessels.name: INS Cuddalore
mine_warfare_vessels.funfact: Named after the port city of Cuddalore.

mine_warfare_vessels.name: INS Pondicherry
mine_warfare_vessels.funfact: Lead ship of Soviet-origin minesweepers. All now decommissioned due to age.

mine_warfare_vessels.name: MCMV Project (New)
mine_warfare_vessels.funfact: India is acquiring modern mine countermeasure vessels to replace aging fleet. Critical for keeping shipping lanes open.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: MiG-29K/KUB
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's only carrier-based fighter jets! Can take off from ski-jump ramps. Equipped with anti-ship missiles and smart bombs.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: Boeing P-8I Poseidon
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's most advanced maritime patrol aircraft! Can detect submarines hundreds of km away. Equipped with magnetic anomaly detector and sonobuoys.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: Ilyushin Il-38
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: Veteran of Indian naval aviation! Upgraded with Sea Dragon systems. Being replaced by P-8I.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: Dornier Do 228
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: Workhorse of coastal surveillance! Can operate from small airstrips. Used extensively for anti-piracy patrols. HAL builds them in India.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: HAL Tejas (Naval variant)
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's first indigenous carrier-based fighter! Single-engine prototype successfully landed on INS Vikrant. Twin-engine version (TEDBF) now under development for future carriers.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: TEDBF (Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter)
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: Next-generation indigenous carrier fighter! Will replace MiG-29K. Features stealth design and advanced avionics. Designed for both ski-jump and CATOBAR operations.

fixed_wing_aircraft.name: Britten-Norman Islander
fixed_wing_aircraft.funfact: Used for inter-island transport in Andaman & Nicobar and Lakshadweep. Can land on short runways.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: Sikorsky MH-60R Seahawk
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's most advanced naval helicopter! $2.4 billion deal signed in 2020. Nicknamed 'Romeo'. Has dipping sonar and advanced radar. Can detect and destroy submarines.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: Kamov Ka-31
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's eyes in the sky! Has a large rotating radar antenna under the fuselage. Can detect aircraft and ships 150 km away. Operates from aircraft carriers and large warships.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: Kamov Ka-28/Ka-28PL
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: Coaxial rotor design allows operations from small ship decks! Doesn't need a tail rotor. Being replaced by MH-60R.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: Westland Sea King Mk.42B/C
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: Veteran of multiple wars! First used Sea Eagle missiles in exercises. Being replaced by MH-60R and indigenous helicopters. Some converted to commando carriers.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: HAL Chetak
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: Legendary workhorse serving for over 60 years! License-built version of French Alouette III. Used for training generations of naval aviators.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: HAL Dhruv (Naval variant)
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's first indigenous helicopter in naval service! Can operate from warship decks. Variants include SAR and utility versions.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: HAL ALH Mk III
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: Advanced variant with Shakti engines and modern avionics. Features rescue hoist and advanced navigation systems.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: HAL NMRH (Naval Multi-Role Helicopter)
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: India's ambitious project to develop indigenous naval helicopter! Will replace Sea Kings and Ka-28s. Based on 12-ton platform.

rotary_wing_aircraft.name: Boeing Chinook CH-47F(I)
rotary_wing_aircraft.quantity: Shared with IAF for naval requirements
rotary_wing_aircraft.funfact: Though operated by IAF, used for naval requirements. Can lift heavy equipment to remote islands and ships.

unmanned_aerial_vehicles.name: IAI Heron
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.funfact: Can patrol vast ocean areas for days! Provides real-time intelligence to fleet commanders. Crucial for maritime domain awareness.

unmanned_aerial_vehicles.name: IAI Searcher Mk II
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.funfact: Workhorse tactical UAV. Can be launched from shore stations for coastal surveillance.

unmanned_aerial_vehicles.name: MQ-9B Sea Guardian
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.type: High Altitude Long Endurance (HALE) Armed UAV
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.funfact: India's first armed maritime drone! Can patrol entire Indian Ocean for days. Will revolutionize maritime surveillance. Part of $3 billion deal with USA.

unmanned_aerial_vehicles.name: DRDO TAPAS/Rustom-2
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.funfact: Indigenous MALE UAV for all three services. Will reduce dependence on imported drones.

unmanned_aerial_vehicles.name: DRDO Abhyas
role: Target practice for missiles and guns
unmanned_aerial_vehicles.funfact: High-speed expendable aerial target. Used for live-fire training of naval air defense systems.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Hansa
naval_air_stations.funfact: India's largest naval air station! Primary base for MiG-29K fighters. Has India's only shore-based ski-jump for carrier pilot training (SBTF).

naval_air_stations.name: INS Dega
naval_air_stations.funfact: Primary air station for Eastern Naval Command. Home to P-8I Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Shikra
naval_air_stations.funfact: Located in Mumbai Harbor. Provides helicopter support to Western Fleet.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Garuda
naval_air_stations.funfact: Oldest operational naval air station! Named after the mythical bird Garuda, vehicle of Lord Vishnu.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Utkrosh
naval_air_stations.funfact: Strategic air station in Andaman Islands. Monitors approaches to Strait of Malacca.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Rajali
naval_air_stations.funfact: India's premier maritime patrol aircraft base! Home to the P-8I Poseidon fleet. Has one of the longest runways in India.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Parundu
naval_air_stations.funfact: Newest naval air facility. UAV operations base for surveillance of Palk Strait.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Baaz
naval_air_stations.funfact: India's southernmost air station! Only 90 nautical miles from Strait of Malacca. Strategic for monitoring one of world's busiest shipping lanes.

naval_air_stations.name: INS Kohassa
naval_air_stations.funfact: Third air station in Andaman & Nicobar. Named after a white-bellied sea eagle found in the islands.

marcos.name: MARCOS (Marine Commandos)

notable_operations.name: Operation Cactus
notable_operations.description: Helped restore government after coup attempt

notable_operations.name: 26/11 Mumbai Attacks
notable_operations.description: Neutralized terrorists at Taj Hotel and Oberoi Hotel

notable_operations.name: Operation Black Tornado
notable_operations.description: NSG-led operation with MARCOS support at multiple sites

notable_operations.name: Anti-Piracy Operations
notable_operations.description: Rescued numerous ships and crews from pirates

marcos.funfact: MARCOS are among the few special forces in the world trained in all three dimensions - land, sea, and air. They undergo training with US Navy SEALs and British SBS. They can hold their breath for over 5 minutes and swim 40 km in open ocean.

diving_teams.name: Naval Clearance Diving Teams
diving_teams.funfact: Navy divers have salvaged aircraft, recovered black boxes, and cleared mines. They can work at depths exceeding 100 meters.

anti_ship_missiles.name: BrahMos
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: World's fastest cruise missile in operational service! Named after Brahmaputra (India) and Moskva (Russia) rivers. Can be launched from vertical launchers. Sea-skimming capability makes it nearly impossible to intercept.

anti_ship_missiles.name: BrahMos-NG (Next Generation)
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: Smaller and lighter than original BrahMos. Will fit in torpedo tubes and aircraft. More missiles per ship.

anti_ship_missiles.name: P-20M Termit (SS-N-2D Styx)
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: Veteran missile! Indian Navy sank Pakistani warships with Styx missiles in 1971. First-ever ship sinking by anti-ship missiles in combat.

anti_ship_missiles.name: Kh-35 Uran (SS-N-25 Switchblade)
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: Sea-skimming missile that flies just meters above water. Difficult to detect by ship radars.

anti_ship_missiles.name: Exocet MM40
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: Submarine-launched Exocets can be fired from torpedo tubes while submerged. Made famous in the Falklands War.

anti_ship_missiles.name: DRDO NASM (Naval Anti-Ship Missile)
anti_ship_missiles.funfact: Indigenous long-range anti-ship missile to reduce import dependency.

surface_to_air_missiles.name: Barak-8 (LR-SAM/MR-SAM)
surface_to_air_missiles.funfact: India's most advanced naval air defense system! Can engage aircraft, helicopters, anti-ship missiles, and UAVs simultaneously. 360-degree coverage. Can engage sea-skimming missiles.

surface_to_air_missiles.name: Barak-1
surface_to_air_missiles.funfact: Fast reaction missile for close-in defense. Can engage multiple threats within seconds.

surface_to_air_missiles.name: Shtil/Shtil-1 (SA-N-7 Gadfly)
surface_to_air_missiles.funfact: Naval version of the Buk missile system. Being replaced by Barak-8 on newer ships.

surface_to_air_missiles.name: 9K32 Strela-2 (SA-N-5 Grail)
surface_to_air_missiles.funfact: Shoulder-launched missile for close-in air defense. Last-ditch weapon against aircraft.

surface_to_air_missiles.name: Igla-S (SA-24 Grinch)
surface_to_air_missiles.funfact: Modern MANPADS with improved guidance. Can engage targets from any direction.

submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.name: K-15 Sagarika
submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.funfact: India's first SLBM! Completes India's nuclear triad. Can be launched from underwater. Named 'Sagarika' meaning 'Born from the Ocean'.

submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.name: K-4
submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.funfact: Can reach any target in Pakistan and most of China from Indian Ocean! Multiple warhead capability (potentially MIRV). Gives India credible second-strike capability.

submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.name: K-5
submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.funfact: Next-generation SLBM with intercontinental range. Will give India global reach from underwater platforms.

submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.name: K-6
submarine_launched_ballistic_missiles.funfact: Future SLBM with multiple independently targetable warheads. Will be deployed on larger S5-class SSBNs.

torpedoes.name: Varunastra
torpedoes.funfact: India's first indigenous heavyweight torpedo! Electric propulsion makes it quiet. Can engage submarines and surface ships. Named after Lord Varuna's weapon.

torpedoes.name: TAL Shyena
torpedoes.funfact: Air-dropped anti-submarine torpedo. 'Shyena' means Hawk in Sanskrit. Indigenous alternative to imported torpedoes.

torpedoes.name: SET-65E
torpedoes.funfact: Soviet-era torpedo still in service on older submarines.

torpedoes.name: TEST-71
torpedoes.funfact: Can be guided by submarine throughout its attack run.

torpedoes.name: SUT (Surface and Underwater Torpedo)
torpedoes.funfact: German wire-guided torpedo. Can attack both submarines and surface ships.

torpedoes.name: Black Shark
torpedoes.funfact: One of the world's most advanced torpedoes. Electric propulsion with very long range.

torpedoes.name: MK 54 Lightweight Torpedo
torpedoes.funfact: Comes with MH-60R helicopters. Can be dropped from helicopters to attack submarines.

guns_and_ciws.name: 76mm Super Rapid Gun Mount (SRGM)
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Versatile gun for anti-ship, anti-aircraft, and shore bombardment. Can fire guided munitions.

guns_and_ciws.name: 100mm AK-100
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Soviet-designed single-barrel gun. Good for shore bombardment.

guns_and_ciws.name: 127mm/54 (5-inch) Gun
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Powerful gun for naval gunfire support and anti-ship warfare.

guns_and_ciws.name: AK-630 CIWS
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Last line of defense against incoming missiles! Gatling-type gun fires a 'wall of lead'. Two usually mounted per ship for 360-degree coverage.

guns_and_ciws.name: Kashtan CIWS
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Unique system combining guns and missiles for close-in defense. Can engage targets from 10 km (missiles) to 500m (guns).

guns_and_ciws.name: CRN-91 (30mm Naval Gun)
guns_and_ciws.funfact: Indigenous stabilized gun system. Can be operated remotely.

anti_submarine_weapons.name: RBU-6000 (Smerch-2)
anti_submarine_weapons.funfact: Fires a pattern of depth charges to blanket submarine positions. Can also be used for torpedo defense.

anti_submarine_weapons.name: Sonobuoys
anti_submarine_weapons.funfact: Dropped from aircraft to detect submarines. Can be passive (listening) or active (pinging).

anti_submarine_weapons.name: Dipping Sonar
anti_submarine_weapons.funfact: Lowered from hovering helicopter to detect submarines. Can pinpoint location for torpedo attack.

historic_operations.name: Operation Trident
historic_operations.result: Sank PNS Khaibar (destroyer), MV Venus Challenger; damaged PNS Shah Jahan
historic_operations.significance: First successful anti-ship missile attack in history!
historic_operations.funfact: This operation is why India celebrates Navy Day on December 4. Changed naval warfare forever by proving effectiveness of missile boats.

historic_operations.name: Operation Python
historic_operations.result: Destroyed oil storage tanks at Keamari, sank multiple vessels
historic_operations.funfact: Karachi's fuel reserves burned for seven days! Completely demoralized Pakistani Navy.

historic_operations.name: Operation Vijay (Naval)
historic_operations.result: Complete naval blockade of East Pakistan, destruction of Chittagong and Cox's Bazar ports
historic_operations.funfact: INS Vikrant's aircraft flew 125+ sorties. Pakistani Navy completely neutralized in Eastern theater. Hastened surrender of Pakistani forces.

historic_operations.name: Operation Cactus
historic_operations.result: Helped restore legitimate Maldivian government after coup attempt
historic_operations.funfact: Indian Navy ships intercepted fleeing mercenaries. Rescued President Gayoom's government within hours of request.

historic_operations.name: Operation Parakram
historic_operations.action: Massive naval mobilization and forward deployment
historic_operations.funfact: Largest naval mobilization since 1971. Demonstrated ability to operate both carriers simultaneously.

historic_operations.name: Operation Sukoon
historic_operations.result: Evacuated 2,280 people (Indians, Sri Lankans, Nepalis, Lebanese)
historic_operations.funfact: Evacuated civilians during Israel-Lebanon War. Ships entered active war zone under fire.

historic_operations.name: Operation Raahat
historic_operations.funfact: Evacuated citizens of 41 countries! Operations conducted under Saudi-led airstrikes. One of the largest evacuation operations by Indian Navy.

historic_operations.name: Operation Samudra Setu
historic_operations.funfact: First large-scale naval repatriation during pandemic. Ships converted to floating hospitals with quarantine facilities.

historic_operations.name: Anti-Piracy Operations
historic_operations.location: Gulf of Aden, Somali Basin, Arabian Sea
historic_operations.action: Continuous deployment of warships for maritime security
historic_operations.funfact: Indian Navy has been continuously deployed since 2008. INS Tabar famously sank a pirate 'mother ship' in 2008. No ship under Indian Navy escort has ever been successfully hijacked.

historic_operations.name: Operation Sankalp
historic_operations.location: Gulf of Oman, Strait of Hormuz
historic_operations.funfact: Ongoing operation ensuring safe passage for Indian oil tankers through the world's most critical oil chokepoint.

under_construction.name: Project 17A (Nilgiri-class Frigates)
under_construction.funfact: Will be the most advanced frigates in Indian Navy. 75% indigenous content.

under_construction.name: Project 15B (Visakhapatnam-class Destroyers)
under_construction.status: Final ship INS Surat in sea trials
under_construction.funfact: Most potent destroyers in the region. Each costs approximately ₹4,000 crores.

under_construction.name: Project 75 (Scorpène-class Submarines)
under_construction.funfact: Last submarine will feature indigenous AIP system.

under_construction.name: Next Generation Missile Vessels (NGMVs)
under_construction.funfact: Stealthy missile boats to replace aging Veer-class. Will carry BrahMos missiles.

under_construction.name: Next Generation Offshore Patrol Vessels
under_construction.funfact: Modern OPVs for enhanced coastal and EEZ patrol.

under_construction.name: Fleet Support Ships (FSS)
under_construction.funfact: Will replace aging tankers. Essential for extended blue-water operations.

under_construction.name: Survey Vessels (New generation)
under_construction.funfact: Modern hydrographic survey vessels. First ship INS Sarvekshak already commissioned.

planned_acquisitions.name: Project 75(I) Submarines
planned_acquisitions.funfact: India's largest defense procurement! Will be built in India with foreign collaboration. Contenders include TKMS (Germany), Naval Group (France), Navantia (Spain), Daewoo (South Korea).

planned_acquisitions.name: Indigenous Aircraft Carrier-2 (IAC-2)
planned_acquisitions.funfact: Will be India's largest warship ever! May feature electromagnetic catapults (EMALS). Can operate heavier aircraft than ski-jump carriers.

planned_acquisitions.name: Project Varsha (Indigenous SSN)
planned_acquisitions.funfact: India's most ambitious submarine project. Will give blue-water attack capability.

planned_acquisitions.name: MH-60R Seahawk Helicopters
planned_acquisitions.funfact: $2.4 billion deal. Replacing aging Sea Kings.

planned_acquisitions.name: TEDBF (Twin Engine Deck Based Fighter)
planned_acquisitions.funfact: Indigenous carrier fighter to replace MiG-29K.

planned_acquisitions.name: Landing Platform Dock (LPD)
planned_acquisitions.funfact: Will significantly enhance amphibious warfare capability.

planned_acquisitions.name: Multi-Purpose Vessel/Landing Helicopter Dock
planned_acquisitions.funfact: Large amphibious assault ship similar to Mistral-class.

planned_acquisitions.name: Mine Counter-Measure Vessels (MCMVs)
planned_acquisitions.funfact: Critical capability gap. Will replace aging minesweepers.

indigenous_programs.name: AIP (Air Independent Propulsion)
indigenous_programs.funfact: Will allow submarines to stay submerged for weeks! Indigenous system reduces foreign dependence. Cheaper than imported systems.

indigenous_programs.name: Integrated Underwater Harbour Defence System
indigenous_programs.funfact: To protect naval harbors from underwater threats including divers and mini-submarines.

indigenous_programs.name: USHUS (Submarine Sonar System)
indigenous_programs.funfact: Indigenous submarine sonar now operational on Indian submarines. Major achievement in sensor technology.

indigenous_programs.name: HUMSA (Hull Mounted Sonar Array)
indigenous_programs.funfact: Indigenous sonar for surface ships. Comparable to imported systems.

indigenous_programs.name: Maareech Torpedo Defense System
indigenous_programs.funfact: Indigenous torpedo decoy system to protect ships from torpedo attack.

training_establishments.name: Indian Naval Academy (INA)
training_establishments.funfact: Asia's largest naval academy! 2,500-acre campus. Trains officers from India and friendly foreign navies. First academy to train all three branches (executive, engineering, electrical) under one roof.

training_establishments.name: INS Chilka
training_establishments.funfact: Asia's largest sailor training establishment. All Indian Navy sailors pass through here.

training_establishments.name: INS Shivaji
training_establishments.funfact: Premier engineering training establishment. Named after Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

training_establishments.name: INS Valsura
training_establishments.funfact: Electrical and electronics training. Named after a local village.

training_establishments.name: INS Venduruthy
training_establishments.funfact: Trains officers in navigation and warfare. Has specialized simulation facilities.

training_establishments.name: INS Dronacharya
training_establishments.funfact: Named after the legendary archery teacher. Trains all weapon systems operators.

training_establishments.name: INS Hamla
training_establishments.funfact: Supply and secretariat training. 'Hamla' means Attack in Hindi.

training_establishments.name: INS Satavahana
training_establishments.funfact: India's submarine school. Named after the Satavahana dynasty which had naval power.

training_establishments.name: INS Agrani
training_establishments.funfact: Leadership and management training. 'Agrani' means Pioneer/Leader.

international_exercises.name: MALABAR
international_exercises.funfact: Started as bilateral India-US exercise in 1992. Now includes all Quad nations. Focuses on high-end warfare including carrier operations, anti-submarine warfare.

international_exercises.name: VARUNA
international_exercises.funfact: Named after the Vedic god of oceans. Includes aircraft carrier operations when possible.

international_exercises.name: KONKAN
international_exercises.funfact: Named after India's Konkan coast. Long-standing exercise with the Royal Navy.

international_exercises.name: INDRA Navy
international_exercises.funfact: One of the oldest naval exercises. Demonstrates India-Russia naval cooperation.

international_exercises.name: SIMBEX
international_exercises.funfact: Singapore-India Maritime Bilateral Exercise. Focuses on advanced naval warfare.

international_exercises.name: CORPAT (Coordinated Patrol)
international_exercises.funfact: Joint patrols of maritime boundaries to prevent smuggling, piracy, and illegal fishing.

international_exercises.name: MILAN
international_exercises.funfact: India hosts this massive multilateral exercise. 'MILAN' means 'Meeting' in Hindi. Over 40 navies participate. Showcases Indian Navy's role as net security provider.

international_exercises.name: RIMPAC
international_exercises.funfact: World's largest naval exercise. India has participated since 2014.

wartime_gallantry.funfact: Awarded for leadership during attack on Karachi harbor.
Many officers and sailors received Vir Chakras during 1971 operations.

peacetime_gallantry.funfact: Awarded for extraordinary bravery in peacetime.
Awarded for conspicuous gallantry otherwise than in the face of the enemy.
Several MARCOS operators have received this for anti-terror operations.

fun_facts_and_records.fact: First Asian Navy to operate an aircraft carrier
fun_facts_and_records.funfact: India commissioned INS Vikrant before China, Japan, or any other Asian nation.

fun_facts_and_records.fact: First successful anti-ship missile attack in history
fun_facts_and_records.funfact: Indian Navy's attack on Karachi changed naval warfare forever.

fun_facts_and_records.name: SMEREX
fun_facts_and_records.funfact: India hosts major submarine rescue exercises, crucial given submarine fleet size.
First Indian all-women crew to circumnavigate the globe! Covered 21,600 nautical miles in 254 days.
Has sailed over 200,000 nautical miles across all major oceans.
Indian Navy has maintained unbroken presence in Gulf of Aden for 15+ years.
Makes India only the 6th country to design and build its own aircraft carrier.
Indian Navy operates the world's fastest cruise missile in operational service.
India became only the 6th nation to achieve operational nuclear triad (land, air, sea-based nuclear weapons).
Can accommodate aircraft carriers and nuclear submarines. Cost over ₹25,000 crores.
